sections.label: Namespace
sections.aiHint: Configures a Kubernetes Namespace to logically isolate resources within a cluster.

fields.label: Namespace name
fields.help: DNS-compatible name. Use lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only.
fields.aiHint: The name of the namespace. Use lowercase kebab-case e.g. production, staging, my-app.

fields.label: Environment label
fields.help: Applied as the 'environment' label on the namespace for filtering and policy targeting.
fields.aiHint: The environment this namespace represents.

sections.label: Service Account
sections.aiHint: Configures a Kubernetes ServiceAccount for pod identity and RBAC. Used for IRSA (AWS) and Workload Identity (GCP).

fields.label: Service account name
fields.help: Name of the ServiceAccount. Referenced in pod spec via serviceAccountName.
fields.aiHint: Name of the service account, typically matching the app name.

fields.label: Namespace
fields.aiHint: Namespace where the service account will be created.

fields.label: Automount API token
fields.help: Set to false to disable automatic token mounting for security. Only enable if the pod needs to call the Kubernetes API.
fields.aiHint: Whether to automatically mount the API token into pods using this service account.

fields.label: Annotation key (e.g. for IRSA)
fields.help: Used to link the service account to a cloud IAM role (AWS IRSA or GCP Workload Identity).
fields.aiHint: Annotation key for linking to a cloud IAM role, such as eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn for AWS.

fields.label: Annotation value
fields.aiHint: The IAM role ARN or service account email to associate with this Kubernetes service account.

sections.label: ConfigMap
sections.aiHint: Stores non-sensitive configuration as key-value pairs, mounted as environment variables or files into pods.

fields.label: ConfigMap name
fields.aiHint: Name of the ConfigMap, typically appended with -config.

fields.label: Namespace
fields.aiHint: Namespace where the ConfigMap will be created.

fields.label: Data (key=value, one per line)
fields.help: One key=value pair per line. Values are stored as plain text — use Secret for sensitive data.
fields.aiHint: Configuration key-value pairs. Each line is one entry in the format KEY=value.

sections.label: Secret
sections.aiHint: Stores sensitive configuration such as passwords, API keys, and TLS certificates. Values are base64-encoded.

fields.label: Secret name
fields.aiHint: Name of the Secret, typically appended with -secret or -credentials.

fields.label: Namespace
fields.aiHint: Namespace where the Secret will be created.

fields.label: Secret type
fields.help: Opaque is the generic type for arbitrary data. Use kubernetes.io/tls for TLS certificates.
fields.aiHint: The type of secret. Use Opaque for generic key-value secrets.

fields.label: Data (key=base64value, one per line)
fields.help: Values must be base64-encoded. Use: echo -n 'myvalue' | base64
fields.aiHint: Secret key-value pairs. Values must be base64-encoded strings.

sections.label: PersistentVolumeClaim
sections.aiHint: Requests persistent storage for stateful workloads such as databases. The cluster provisions a PersistentVolume to satisfy the claim.

fields.label: PVC name
fields.aiHint: Name of the PersistentVolumeClaim, typically appended with -pvc.

fields.label: Namespace
fields.aiHint: Namespace where the PVC will be created.

fields.label: Storage class
fields.help: Leave blank to use the cluster default storage class. Common values: standard, gp2, gp3, premium-rwo.
fields.aiHint: Storage class to use. Leave blank for default. Use gp2 or gp3 for AWS EKS, standard for GKE.

fields.label: Access mode
fields.help: ReadWriteOnce (RWO) is supported by most block storage providers and is sufficient for most stateful apps.
fields.aiHint: How the volume can be mounted. ReadWriteOnce is standard for single-pod databases.

fields.label: Storage size
fields.help: Size in Kubernetes resource format. e.g. 1Gi, 10Gi, 100Gi, 500Mi
fields.aiHint: Storage size in Gi or Mi format e.g. 10Gi for 10 gigabytes.

sections.label: Deployment
sections.aiHint: Defines the desired state for a set of identical pods. Manages rolling updates, scaling, and self-healing.

fields.label: App name
fields.help: Used as the Deployment name and as the selector label. Must be DNS-compatible.
fields.aiHint: Name of the application. Used as the deployment name, pod label, and selector.

fields.label: Namespace
fields.aiHint: Namespace where the Deployment will be created.

fields.label: Replicas
fields.help: Number of pod replicas to maintain. For HA, use at least 2. If HPA is enabled this acts as the initial count.
fields.aiHint: Number of pod replicas. Use 2 or more for high availability.

fields.label: Container image
fields.help: Full image reference including tag. Always pin a specific tag in production — never use :latest.
fields.aiHint: Docker image including tag e.g. nginx:1.25, my-app:v1.0.0, gcr.io/project/app:sha256.

fields.label: Container port
fields.help: Port the application listens on inside the container.
fields.aiHint: The port the application listens on inside the container.

fields.label: CPU request
fields.help: Guaranteed CPU. Format: millicores (100m = 0.1 CPU) or whole cores (1). Scheduler uses this for placement.
fields.aiHint: CPU resources guaranteed to the container. 100m means 0.1 CPU core.

fields.label: CPU limit
fields.help: Maximum CPU the container may use. Throttled if exceeded. Set higher than request.
fields.aiHint: Maximum CPU the container can use before being throttled.

fields.label: Memory request
fields.help: Guaranteed memory. Format: Mi (mebibytes) or Gi (gibibytes). Pod is evicted if it exceeds its limit.
fields.aiHint: Memory guaranteed to the container e.g. 128Mi, 256Mi, 512Mi, 1Gi.

fields.label: Memory limit
fields.help: Maximum memory. Container is OOMKilled if it exceeds this. Set higher than request.
fields.aiHint: Maximum memory the container can use before being killed.

fields.label: ConfigMap ref (envFrom)
fields.help: Name of a ConfigMap to load as environment variables via envFrom. Must exist in the same namespace.
fields.aiHint: Name of a ConfigMap to inject as environment variables into the container.

fields.label: Secret ref (envFrom)
fields.help: Name of a Secret to load as environment variables via envFrom. Must exist in the same namespace.
fields.aiHint: Name of a Secret to inject as environment variables into the container.

fields.label: Volume mount name
fields.help: Name of the volume to mount. Must match a PVC name or emptyDir volume defined in the spec.
fields.aiHint: Name of the volume to mount into the container. References a PVC or emptyDir.

fields.label: Volume mount path
fields.help: Directory inside the container where the volume is mounted.
fields.aiHint: File system path inside the container where the volume will be mounted e.g. /data, /var/lib/postgresql.

fields.label: Liveness probe path
fields.help: HTTP GET path for liveness probe. Container is restarted if this returns non-2xx. Leave blank to disable.
fields.aiHint: HTTP endpoint for liveness check e.g. /health, /ping, /actuator/health.

fields.label: Liveness probe port
fields.aiHint: Port to use for the liveness probe HTTP request.

fields.label: Readiness probe path
fields.help: HTTP GET path for readiness probe. Pod only receives traffic when this returns 2xx.
fields.aiHint: HTTP endpoint for readiness check. Pod only receives traffic when this passes.

fields.label: Readiness probe port
fields.aiHint: Port to use for the readiness probe HTTP request.

fields.label: Restart policy
fields.help: Always is correct for Deployments. OnFailure/Never are for Jobs.
fields.aiHint: When to restart containers. Always is standard for long-running services.

fields.label: Image pull policy
fields.help: IfNotPresent is recommended for pinned tags. Use Always only with :latest (which itself is not recommended).
fields.aiHint: When to pull the container image from the registry.

fields.label: Service account name
fields.help: ServiceAccount to assign to pods. Leave blank to use the namespace default.
fields.aiHint: Name of the ServiceAccount to use for pods in this deployment.

fields.label: Update strategy
fields.help: RollingUpdate replaces pods gradually with zero downtime. Recreate terminates all pods before starting new ones.
fields.aiHint: How pods are updated. RollingUpdate is zero-downtime; Recreate is simpler but causes downtime.

fields.label: Max surge
fields.help: Max extra pods above desired replica count during rolling update. Can be a number or percentage e.g. 25%
fields.aiHint: Maximum extra pods allowed during a rolling update.

fields.label: Max unavailable
fields.help: Max pods that can be unavailable during rolling update. 0 means always maintain full replica count.
fields.aiHint: Maximum number of pods that can be unavailable during a rolling update.

sections.label: Service
sections.aiHint: Exposes the Deployment as a network service within the cluster or externally.

fields.label: Service name
fields.help: DNS name used to reach the service from within the cluster.
fields.aiHint: Name of the Service. Used as the DNS name within the cluster.

fields.label: Namespace
fields.aiHint: Namespace where the Service will be created. Must match the Deployment namespace.

fields.label: Service type
fields.help: ClusterIP: internal only. NodePort: exposed on each node. LoadBalancer: creates a cloud load balancer.
fields.aiHint: How the service is exposed. ClusterIP for internal, LoadBalancer for public internet access.

fields.label: Service port
fields.help: Port exposed on the Service. Clients connect to this port.
fields.aiHint: Port that clients connect to on the Service.

fields.label: Target port
fields.help: Port on the container the service forwards to. Must match the container port in the Deployment.
fields.aiHint: Port on the container to forward traffic to. Must match the container port.

fields.label: Selector app label
fields.help: Must match the app label on the pods in the target Deployment.
fields.aiHint: Must exactly match the app_name field in the Deployment to route traffic correctly.

fields.label: Node port (NodePort/LoadBalancer only)
fields.help: Static port on each node. Must be in range 30000-32767. Leave blank for auto-assignment.
fields.aiHint: Static node port in range 30000-32767. Leave blank to auto-assign.

fields.label: Session affinity
fields.help: ClientIP pins a client to the same pod for the session duration. Use for stateful apps without external session storage.
fields.aiHint: None distributes traffic freely. ClientIP sticks a client to the same pod.

sections.label: Ingress
sections.aiHint: Routes external HTTP/HTTPS traffic to Services based on host and path rules. Requires an Ingress Controller installed in the cluster.

fields.label: Ingress name
fields.aiHint: Name of the Ingress resource.

fields.label: Namespace
fields.aiHint: Namespace where the Ingress will be created.

fields.label: Ingress class
fields.help: Must match the ingress controller installed in your cluster.
fields.aiHint: The ingress controller to use. nginx is most common.

fields.label: Hostname
fields.help: Fully qualified domain name. Must be configured in DNS to point to the ingress controller.
fields.aiHint: The domain name for the application e.g. myapp.example.com.

fields.label: Path
fields.aiHint: URL path prefix to route e.g. / for all traffic or /api for API routes only.

fields.label: Path type
fields.help: Prefix matches any path starting with the specified prefix. Exact matches only the exact path.
fields.aiHint: How the path is matched. Prefix for most use cases.

fields.label: Backend service name
fields.help: Must match the Service name in the same namespace.
fields.aiHint: Name of the Service to route traffic to. Must exist in the same namespace.

fields.label: Backend service port
fields.aiHint: Port on the Service to route traffic to.

fields.label: Enable TLS
fields.help: Enables HTTPS. Requires a TLS Secret in the same namespace with the certificate and key.
fields.aiHint: Enable HTTPS termination at the ingress.

fields.label: TLS secret name
fields.help: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the TLS certificate and key. Type must be kubernetes.io/tls.
fields.aiHint: Name of the Secret containing the TLS certificate and private key.

sections.label: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
sections.aiHint: Automatically scales the number of pod replicas based on CPU or memory utilisation.

fields.label: HPA name
fields.aiHint: Name of the HorizontalPodAutoscaler resource.

fields.label: Namespace
fields.aiHint: Namespace where the HPA will be created.

fields.label: Target deployment name
fields.help: Must match the name of the Deployment this HPA scales.
fields.aiHint: The name of the Deployment to scale. Must match the app_name in the Deployment section.

fields.label: Min replicas
fields.help: Minimum number of pods. Should be at least 2 for HA.
fields.aiHint: Minimum number of pods to keep running. Use 2 or more for high availability.

fields.label: Max replicas
fields.help: Maximum pods the HPA will scale to. Keep this bounded to control cloud costs.
fields.aiHint: Maximum number of pods the autoscaler can create.

fields.label: CPU target utilisation (%)
fields.help: Scale up when average CPU across all pods exceeds this percentage. 70% is a good starting point.
fields.aiHint: Target CPU utilisation percentage to trigger scaling. 70 is a sensible default.

fields.label: Memory target utilisation (%)
fields.help: Scale up when average memory across all pods exceeds this percentage. Leave blank to disable memory-based scaling.
fields.aiHint: Target memory utilisation percentage. Leave blank to scale on CPU only.
